key_insight: FEMONet learns complex optical scattering by combining operator learning with finite-element Galerkin constraints, so predictions live in a physically consistent solution space instead of unconstrained field values.
engineering_relevance: This enables faster, more generalizable full-wave nanophotonic simulation for forward analysis and inverse design across diverse structures, including 3D and plasmonic geometries.
method: Encodes the wave problem in an operator parameter space of physical entities.
Uses the variational weak form and finite-element discretization to map to Galerkin-consistent solution coefficients.
Removes coordinate derivatives from the physics loss by assembling stiffness matrices and load vectors.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports high accuracy, stable training, and strong generalization across dielectric, metallic, arrayed, plasmonic, and three-dimensional nanophotonic structures.
watch_item: The approach depends on finite-element discretization and compatible weak-form assembly, so implementation quality and mesh/discretization choices may strongly affect performance.
paper_title: Learning light scattering from operator parameter spaces to Galerkin-consistent solution spaces
